01_initial_request.input_data: Celastrol, Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected C57BL/6 mouse model, Plasmodium falciparum in vitro culture, Cel activity probe
01_initial_request.user_intent: To elucidate the antimalarial mechanism of Celastrol and identify its specific protein targets to address drug resistance
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Verification of binding to critical target proteins and demonstration of favorable antimalarial activity in vivo and in vitro

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Malaria control is challenged by drug resistance. Celastrol (Cel) shows antimalarial activity but mechanism is unknown. [Gap] In vivo efficacy and safety profile of Cel need systematic evaluation. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected mouse model] to perform [antimalarial activity assessment] on [C57BL/6 mice], expecting to obtain [parasitemia reduction and organ protection data].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Significant reduction in blood parasitemia, reduced organ enlargement, decreased inflammatory cell infiltration, and ameliorated liver injury (ALT/AST levels).

references.citation: Peters W, et al. Ann Trop Med Parasitol. 1975
references.relevance: Describes the chemotherapy of rodent malaria model used.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] In vivo results suggest efficacy. [Gap] In vitro potency against human malaria parasite P. falciparum needs confirmation. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [SYBR Green I assay] to perform [antimalarial activity measurement] on [P. falciparum 3D7 and Dd2 strains], expecting to obtain [IC50 values].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cel showed favorable antimalarial activity in low uM concentration range.

references.citation: Gao P, et al. Mil Med Res. 2022
references.relevance: Describes the SYBR Green I assay method for antimalarial activity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Efficacy confirmed. [Gap] Specific protein targets of Cel in the parasite are unknown. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [In situ fluorescence labeling] to perform [target profiling] on [P. falciparum proteins] using [Cel activity probe (Cel-P)], expecting to obtain [fluorescent labeling patterns].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cel-P labeled parasite proteins in a dose-dependent manner; labeling attenuated by excess Cel.

references.citation: Luo P, et al. Acta Pharm Sin B. 2022
references.relevance: Describes the Cel activity probe development and labeling.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Labeling confirms target engagement. [Gap] Identity of labeled proteins needs determination. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [LC-MS/MS] to perform [protein identification] on [enriched proteins from pull-down], expecting to obtain [list of potential target proteins].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 31 potential target proteins identified, including PfSpdsyn and PfEGF1-α.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Targets identified. [Gap] Global proteome changes induced by Cel need analysis. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [LC-MS/MS (Proteomics)] to perform [differential protein expression analysis] on [Cel-treated parasites], expecting to obtain [differentially expressed proteins].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 1220 proteins showed significant changes; involved in protein synthesis processes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteome changes observed. [Gap] Transcriptome level changes need investigation. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [RNA-sequencing] to perform [gene expression analysis] on [Cel-treated parasites], expecting to obtain [differentially expressed genes].

[Background] PfSpdsyn identified as target. [Gap] Recombinant protein needed for binding validation. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [E. coli expression system] to perform [protein expression and purification] on [PfSpdsyn and PfEGF1-α genes], expecting to obtain [purified recombinant proteins].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recombinant PfSpdsyn and PfEGF1-α proteins expressed and purified.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Recombinant PfSpdsyn available. [Gap] Direct binding of Cel to PfSpdsyn needs verification. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Fluorescence labeling] to perform [binding assay] on [recombinant PfSpdsyn], expecting to obtain [dose-dependent labeling signal].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cel-P binds PfSpdsyn dose-dependently; competed by Cel and IAA.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fluorescence suggests binding. [Gap] Thermal stability change upon binding needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Cellular thermal shift assay (CETSA-WB)] to perform [stability measurement] on [PfSpdsyn with Cel], expecting to obtain [thermal shift data].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cel significantly increased thermal stability of PfSpdsyn.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Stability shift observed. [Gap] Binding affinity kinetics need quantification. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Bio-Layer Interferometry] to perform [affinity measurement] on [PfSpdsyn and Cel], expecting to obtain [binding kinetics].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Interaction of Cel with PfSpdsyn protein demonstrated.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding confirmed. [Gap] Molecular binding mode and sites need prediction. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [MOE] to perform [molecular docking simulation] on [PfSpdsyn structure and Cel], expecting to obtain [potential binding sites].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cysteines 165 and 266 identified as potential binding sites.

references.citation: Gao P, et al. Chin Chem Lett. 2023

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Docking predicts Cys sites. [Gap] Predicted sites need experimental validation. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Fluorescence labeling] to perform [mutant analysis] on [PfSpdsyn mutants (C165A, C266A)], expecting to obtain [labeling efficiency on mutants].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Labeling attenuated on mutants, validating binding sites.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding sites validated. [Gap] Functional consequence on spermidine synthesis needs measurement. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Spermidine ELISA] to perform [metabolite level measurement] on [Cel-treated parasites], expecting to obtain [spermidine concentration].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Spermidine levels decreased in a dose-dependent manner.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PfEGF1-α identified as target. [Gap] Binding of Cel to PfEGF1-α needs verification. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Bio-Layer Interferometry] to perform [affinity measurement] on [PfEGF1-α and Cel], expecting to obtain [binding confirmation].

parameters.settings: Similar to PfSpdsyn (60s baseline, 90s assoc, 120s dissoc)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PfEGF1-α binding confirmed. [Gap] Functional impact on protein synthesis needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve [goal], chose to use [Newly synthesized protein inhibition assay] to perform [translation measurement] on [Cel-treated parasites], expecting to obtain [protein synthesis levels].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cel interfered with de novo protein synthesis of P. falciparum.

references.relevance: Describes quantitative chemical proteomics profiling of protein synthesis.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Binding assays (BLI, CETSA, Fluorescence), Functional assays (Spermidine ELISA, Protein synthesis inhibition), In vivo/In vitro efficacy models

in_vivo_efficacy.interpretation: Cel treatment reduced blood parasitemia similar to artesunate.

in_vitro_potency.interpretation: Cel showed favorable antimalarial activity against P. falciparum.

target_identification.interpretation: 31 potential targets identified, including PfSpdsyn and PfEGF1-α.

binding_affinity.interpretation: Direct binding verified by BLI and CETSA.

functional_impact.interpretation: Cel inhibited spermidine synthesis and protein synthesis.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. Celastrol's antimalarial mechanism was elucidated by verifying binding to PfSpdsyn and PfEGF1-α, demonstrating interference with spermidine and protein synthesis, and confirming favorable antimalarial activity in vivo and in vitro.
